name: Before/After Carousel
description: Editorial before/after carousel framed as a long-form case study: an optional project meta strip (client/scope/year/team), a 16:10 comparison stage with a draggable reveal handle and a side caption that carries the slide title, body copy, and a structured notes block, plus an optional pull-quote, outcome metrics band, and closing CTA. Pointer drag and keyboard arrows (Home/End for ends, arrows in 4% increments) move the reveal; pagination dots and prev/next chevrons advance between cases with a soft slide+fade transition.

descriptions.descriptive: The active slide is a 16:10 surface with the after image full-bleed and the before image clipped from the left by a draggable handle. On lg screens the slide splits into an 8/12 image stage and a 4/12 caption column carrying the slide index, optional location/year, title, description paragraph, and an optional definition-list of project notes (Scope / Crew / Materials / Outcome). Above the carousel, an optional 4-up project meta strip frames the section as a single engagement; below it, an optional pull-quote bridges into a dark metrics band and a closing CTA, so the component can carry an entire case-study section on its own rather than feel like an embedded widget.
descriptions.usage: Use for any 'we changed this' portfolio that needs editorial depth — interior renovations, brand refreshes, logo redesigns, before-and-after marketing visuals. The optional project meta strip, per-slide notes, pull-quote, metrics band and CTA let the section stand in for a full case-study page. Place after a hero or below a stats panel, never alongside another image-heavy carousel on the same page.
descriptions.audienceFit: Architecture and interior design firms, restoration trades, branding agencies, dental and medical aesthetic clinics, photo retouchers, and marketing agencies whose case studies live on transformation imagery.
